text: The patient is positioned in Lloyd Davis with the surgeon and assistant on the right side. In the Trendelenburg position, the small bowel is lifted from the pelvis, exposing the mesosigmoid. A window is created in the sigmoid colon mesentery, and the bowel is divided distally using an endo-GIA stapler. The N-seal articulating vessel sealing device is used for dissection, allowing for better retraction and angle adjustment. Median and lateral dissections are alternated to mobilize the sigmoid colon.

The surgeon progressively lifts the sigmoid colon while carefully protecting the gonadal vessels and the left ureter. The surgical team approaches the splenic flexure, preserving a splenunculus present in the patient. Medial access to the lesser sac is utilized to fully mobilize the splenic flexure.

The surgeon progressively divides the transverse mesocolon and detaches the gastric attachments, fully mobilizing the hepatic flexure of the colon. The specimen is lifted from the duodenum, and the right colon is mobilized laterally. The peritoneum is incised at the pelvic brim to lift the terminal ileum, with the operative field positioned beneath the single incision port at the ileostomy site.
The sealed port is removed, and the surgical specimen is extracted through the ileostomy site. The terminal ileum is divided extracorporeally, and an end-ileostomy is matured.
